response.reasoning: 1. Experience duration: Freelance work begins 2006, formal roles from 2009–present — approximately 17 years of software experience, satisfying the 15-year minimum. 2. Education: MIT BS in Computer Science meets the minimum threshold; no graduate AI degree. 3. AI relevance: Current re:cinq role (April 2025–present) includes AI-agent infrastructure and MCP tooling, but this is a recent pivot (~1 year) and scoped to developer tooling and agentic workflow plumbing — not demonstrated AI product strategy, platform-scale ML, or recognized AI expertise. 4. Cloud expertise: AWS CDK appears briefly at Optilyz; no evidence of deep cloud architecture, cloud platform strategy, or enterprise cloud advisory work. 5. Seniority and scope: All titles sit at Senior or Staff IC level. There is no evidence of director-level scope, shaping cross-org engineering strategy, or advising enterprise customers and partners. 6. Entrepreneurial signal: GISCollective is a genuine founder story, but it is a small open-source geo-data project serving NGOs — not a scaled venture within a larger organization. 7. Community presence: Workshops and a FOSS4G talk demonstrate knowledge sharing, but within niche communities, not broad AI strategy thought leadership. 8. The role's core responsibilities — advising strategic customers on cloud and AI, identifying future cloud product states, inspiring large internal and external audiences — have no clear evidence base in this résumé. Overall, the candidate is a well-rounded senior IC with real breadth and longevity but falls significantly short on AI depth, cloud platform expertise, and director-level strategic scope.
strengths: 17+ years of broad, polyglot engineering experience across web, mobile, embedded, and infrastructure domains satisfies the tenure requirement with headroom.
Active founder of GISCollective and contributor to AI-agent/MCP tooling at re:cinq signals genuine entrepreneurial drive and early-mover interest in agentic AI workflows.
Consistent track record of knowledge sharing — workshops, conference talks, internal dojos — demonstrates the communication and mentorship instincts the role values.
concerns: AI expertise is shallow and nascent: the only AI work is ~1 year of developer tooling at re:cinq; no demonstrated AI products at scale, no ML/AI research background, and no recognized external AI profile.
No evidence of director-level IC scope, enterprise customer advisory, or org-wide strategy influence — all roles are hands-on Senior/Staff engineering positions with limited strategic remit.
Cloud depth is thin: AWS CDK appears once in a supporting role; no cloud platform architecture, large-scale cloud strategy, or enterprise cloud advisory experience is present.
response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a credible senior IC with genuine breadth and longevity, but the Principal Engineer — AI Strategy role is a director-level position requiring deep, recognized AI expertise, cloud platform strategy experience, and the ability to advise enterprise customers — none of which are substantiated in this résumé. The AI work is a recent, narrow pivot into developer tooling rather than strategic product leadership, and the absence of any director-level scope or customer advisory history makes the gap too wide to bridge at this level.
